s.f. A sack, a bag (of coarse cloth); one of the sacks fastened on the sides of a beast of burden (to carry grain in).
more: n. f. S. गोणी. A sack; pannier. Jab marg phirā-kar chābuk ko yeh bail badan kā hāṅkegā. Koī nāj sameṭegā terā, koī gon sīye aur ṭāṅkegā . On death. Banjāre-nāma. Nazīr. When death on high his whip doth crack and make this ox-like body go, Some other will your grain collect, some other then your bags will sew.
s. f. A sack, bag (of coarse cloth), the sacks fastened on the sides of a beast of burthen to carry grain in. ¶ gawan (for गमन ) s. m. Going, moving.

def: s.f. = goćṛī, q.v. s.f. (dialec.), A tick, a cattle-louse.
s.f. = goṅdnī, 'a reed or rush,' q.v. s.f. A kind of reed or rush (the leaves of which are plaited or woven into mats or baskets), Typha angustifolia.
s.m. A low tribe of mountaineers (supposed to be an aboriginal race) inhabiting the Vindhya range in the Central Provinces of India; a man of that tribe, a Goṇḍ.
s.f. Gum; — (dialec.) a kind of gelatinous sweetmeat
more: n. m. S. गुन्द Gum. goṅd paṅjīrī, goṅd makhāne , n. f. A strengthening preparation given to lying-in-women and distributed among her female relations and friends. Das din tāīṅ, ḍūm bulā-kar ḍholak ḍhap bajvāūṅgī , Rus. Wom. Sāthan ke mere chhoraṭ jāmāṅ, gūṅd panjīrī khāūṅgī! Rus. Wom. Musicians I'll call for ten days, their drums shall music make, To my friend a son is born, I'll eat panjīrī cake. goṅd-dānī , n. f. A gum-pot.
s. f. Gum. گوند داني gond-dānī, s. f. A vessel in which gum is kept to seal letters with. گوندکش gond-kash, An instrument for spreading gum with (in sealing letters).
s. (dakh.) A kind of sweetmeat ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xlii).
subs: s.f. A strengthening preparation given to lying-in women (and also distributed among her female relations and friends)
s.m. An instrument for spreading gum with; a gum-brush.

ety: prob. = gūṅdā, perf. part. of gūṅdnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Soft dough (made of the meal of parched gram, &c.) for feeding birds.
more: n. m. 1. A soft dough with which birds are fed, esp. gul-dum . 2. A sweetmeat. goṅdā dikhānā , v. a. To hold up a lump of dough as a bait before a bird. ☞ To excite birds to fight some dough is shown to one and given to another, whereupon a fight ensues.

n. m. 1. A village (گانو). 2. A country road; cart-road. Dhagṛoṅ se tū phire lipaṭtī galiyāre aur goṅḍe māṅ, Buḍḍhā, bālā, koī na chhoḍā, khāk paṛe tere chauṅḍe māṅ! Play. You wander in the streets embracing all the town, Nor old nor young you spare, down to the dust your crown! 3. A yard. 4. (Hin) Alms given to beggars on the arrival of the marriage procession at the bride's village. gauṅḍā sejnā , v. a. To distribute alms to beggars at a marriage. gauṅḍe-vāliyāṅ , n. f. Country women collected at a marriage.
s. m. A thin dough or pap made of the flour of Cicer arietinum, for feeding birds.

def: s.f. = gopī, q.v. s.f. The wife of a gop or cow-herd; a cowherdess (esp. applied to the cowherdesses of Vrindā-van, the companions of Krishṇa's juvenile sports); a milk-maid, dairy-maid
subs: s.m. A species of white clay (said to be brought from Duārakā, and used by Vishṇu's worshippers for smearing their faces)
s.m. 'Lord of the milk-maids,' an epithet of Krishṇa.

def: Sant. n. m. S. गोफणा A sling, esp. one used in a field of corn to drive away cattle, birds, etc. gopiyā phirānā, paṭkhānā, yā chalānā , v. a. To scare birds, etc. from a standing crop by discharging pellets from a sling.

part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), To be protected or preserved; to be taken care of; to be cherished; to be kept secret or hidden; — anything which it is right, lawful, or possible to protect, or cherish, or keep secret or concealed.
s.f. A grave, tomb; in P. also 'a desert')
more: n. f. lit. a waste. See قبر gor banānā , v. n. 1. To dig a grave. 2. To work one's destruction. gor-/?/khar , n. m. A wild ass; an onager. gor-i-garībāṅ , A burying ground for strangers or for the poor. gor kā muṅh jhāṅk-kar phirnā, gor meṅ lāt mār-kar khaṛā h. , v. n. To come out of the jaws of death. gor kafan , Funeral ceremonies. gor-kan , n. m. A grave-digger. gor gaṛhā k. , v. a. To perform the funeral obsequies. lab-i-gor h . To be on the brink of the grave. dar-gor! Mah. Wom. intj. Go to Hell! go and be d—d! dar gor k . v. a. ( Contemp .) To bury one.

1. s. f. A tomb, grave. گور پر گور کرني gor par gor karnī, To seek an employment, office or place which is already occupied. گور غريبان gor-i-g̠ẖarībān, A burying ground for strangers or for the poor. گورکن gor-kan, A grave-digger. 2. s. m. The wild ass: see گورخر gor-ḵẖar.
subs: (-kī), To make a grave (for); — to work the destruction (of)
'To make a grave on a grave'; to seek an employment, office, or place which is already occupied
(or ḵẖar-ě-gor), or gor, s.m. 'The ass of the desert,' the wild ass, the onager (cf. S. gaur-khar)
s.f. Burying-ground for strangers or for the poor
or gor-meṅ lāt-mār-kar khaṛā honā, To escape from the jaws of death
(-kī), To dig a grave (for); to dig up dead bodies
adv. On the brink of the grave.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گور (gōr), from Middle Persian gpl, gwbl (gabr, “hollow, cavity, womb”).

ety: Ap. Prk. गवडु, Prk. गमडो; S. गम+रः
def: The leg; foot; knee
subs: The leg to break or to be broken; — the legs to pain, or to give way (through fatigue, &c.); — (fig.) to have all spirit taken out of one; to be disappointed or discouraged
or gor paṛnā (-ke), To touch the feet (of a person) reverentially; to fall at the feet of.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of fair complexion, fair, white; — red (when applied to cattle); — an epithet of Śiva; — a European soldier, a private; — a European (who is not a gentleman)
more: adj. 1. Fair; white. Kuchh nahīṅ kām hai liyāqat kā, Martabah hai to gorī rangat kā . No need here of ability, for fair skins sole all dignity. Oudh Punch. 2. Beautiful; handsome. gorā , n. m. 1. A European soldier; a private. 2. A European who is not a gentleman. gor'ī , Poet. n. f. A beauty; a belle. Gorī ke nainā aise jaise barad ke sīṅg . Rus. My mistress' eyes as sharp as bullock's horns.

n. m. S. कुपर 1. The leg. 2. The knee; knee pan. goṛe ṭūṭnā , v. n. 1. To be broken (the leg). 2. To be too tired to walk. 3. To be disappointed, discouraged. goṛe paṛnā, yā lagnā , Wom., v. n. To touch one's feet reverentially. Goṛ lāge, māhrāj! goṛe pasārnā , v. n. 1. To stretch one's legs. 2. To die; expire. 3. To lead an idle life.
adj. Fair (complexioned). ¶ गौरा gaurā, s. f. A name of the goddess Pārvatī: see گوري gaurī.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑁄𑀭 (gora), from Sanskrit गौर (gaurá).

def: s.m. The leg, foot ( = goṛ); — a cord or rope for tying the legs of an animal; a fetter; tether
subs: or goṛe lagnā (-ke) = goṛ lagnā, &c., q.v.s.v. goṛ
(apne), To stretch out the legs; — to die; — to lead an idle life.

def: adj. (dakh.) Red and white.
s.m. A corr. form of gorakh-dhandhā.
s. m. (dakh.) 1. Living, life: see زندگاني zinda-gānī. 2. Distraction, alarm, disquietude: see تشويش tashwīsh.
s.m.(?) Name of a clan of Rājpūts in the Agra district.
s.m. = goṛait, q.v.
more: n. m. A village watchman or informer.

ety: or gorait; prob. S. घोर (rt. घुर्)+ait = Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्; — cf. gohrānā or gohārna
def: s.m. A village watchman (the watchman being in the habit of bawling aloud at night); an intelligencer.

s.m. = gorakh-dhandhā, q.v. s.m. Living, way of living; — a troublesome or trying life or existence, distraction, disquietude; — an intricate or difficult affair or problem; a puzzle; a labyrinth or maze; — a Chinese puzzle (consisting of a number of pins passed through holes in an oblong plate; the pins having rings at one end through which an oblong ring is passed); — an intricate kind of lock, a puzzle-lock (Pers. syn. qufl-ě-waswās); — a kind of child's toy, having a string attached to it, which, when extended in one direction turns out to be of one colour; and when extended in another direction, turns out to be of another colour; — any complicated or puzzling plaything, or machine, &c.
more: n. m. 1. An intricate or difficult affair or problem. 2. A labyrinth; maze. 3. A Chinese puzzle; a puzzle lock, etc., a complicated machine.

def: s.m. = gorakh-dhandhā, q.v. s.m. ety: gorakh, prob. a proper name = S. गो+रक्षः+dhandhā, q.v.

Living, way of living; — a troublesome or trying life or existence, distraction, disquietude; — an intricate or difficult affair or problem; a puzzle; a labyrinth or maze; — a Chinese puzzle (consisting of a number of pins passed through holes in an oblong plate; the pins having rings at one end through which an oblong ring is passed); — an intricate kind of lock, a puzzle-lock (Pers. syn. qufl-ě-waswās); — a kind of child's toy, having a string attached to it, which, when extended in one direction turns out to be of one colour; and when extended in another direction, turns out to be of another colour; — any complicated or puzzling plaything, or machine, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) Destroyer of a religious faith.

ety: gorā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Fairness (of complexion); whiteness (of colour) mixed with red; ruddiness.
s. (dakh.) Partridge.

n. m. Z. gavars . Milk, curds, etc. goras'ā ; Bhoj. gorsāh , n. A child brought up on cow's milk. gauṛ'hī , n. m. Pasturage. gaū-sālā, gavārī , n. m. A cow-shed. gaū-kos , n. m. A land measure, being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard.
more: s. m. Milk, butter-milk, curdled milk.

def: s.m. See s.v. go. s.m.f. Water; — s.m. Name of a Rishi; — the sacrifice of a cow; — the thunder-bolt; — the sky; heaven, paradise; — the sun; the moon; a member (of the body); — hair (of the body); — s.f. Speech; Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech; — the earth, the world; a mother; — a quarter of the horizon (as east, west, &c.); — the eye; — an arrow; — adj. Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)
more: 1. adj. (A child) reared with the milk of cattle. 2. s. m. A sodomite.
subs: vulg. gavāksh, gawāććh (˚va+ak˚), s.m. 'A bull's-eye'; an air-hole; a loop-hole, a skylight; a window; lattice; — the light admitted by a window
s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow
s.m. (f. -ī), Cow-keeper, cowherd; — ʻa protector of the earth,' a king; — the cowherd Kr̤ishṇa (one of the epithets of Kr̤ishṇ); — a proper name (of men)
s.m. Title of an Upanishad in honour of Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Having the charge of kine; — protecting the earth; — a cowherd; — a king
s.m. 'The lord of a herd of cows,' a bull
s.m. 'Son of a cow;' a simpleton; a fool
vulg. go-pasu, s.m. A sacrificial cow
vulg. go-pasutā, s.f. The condition of a cow about to be immolated
or gau-thān, s.m. The place where the cattle of a village are assembled; a station for cattle; a cow-pen ( = go-sthān)

adj. & s.m. Ranged or grazed over by cattle; — offering range (or field or scope) for action; within the range (of), accessible, attainable; within the power (of); within sight (of), visible, perceptible; perceived, known; — the range of cattle, pasturage, pasture-ground; range or reach (of the eye, or the understanding, &c.); — the range (or the conjunction or influence) of the planets; — an object of sense (as sound, shape, &c.); — an object of devotion; — perception; information acquired by the perception of the senses
or gau-dān, s.m. The gift of a cow; an offering of a cow
s.m. A cow's tooth; — yellow orpiment; — a white fossil substance (apparently an earthy salt)
s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds
s.f. 'Dust of the earth'; sunset (the time at which mist seems to rise from the earth); evening, twilight
s.m. Cow's milk; buttermilk; curdled or coagulated milk
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī). Reared on cow's milk; one who is reared on cow's milk, a child; — a sodomite
s.f. A vessel for holding milk, &c.; a milk-pail

s.m., or go-roćanā, s.f. A bright yellow pigment prepared from the urine or the bile of a cow, or vomited in the shape of scibulœ by that animal (it is employed in painting and dyeing, and is of especial use in marking the foreheads of Hindūs with the tilak or sectarial mark; it is also used in medicine as a sedative, tonic, and anthelmintic remedy, &c.)
or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

(gosh = gos, gen. of go), s.m. A cow's foot or hoof; — mark or impression of a cow's foot; — puddle or water sufficient to fill a cow's foot-mark; a small puddle; — a measure of capacity (as much as a cow's foot-print would hold)
vulg. goshṭh, s.m. An abode for cattle; cow-house, cow-pen; — any place where animals are kept; — a station of cowherds
s.f. An assembly, a meeting; a society, an association; a confederacy; — family connexions (esp. the dependant or junior branches); — conversation, discourse, dialogue; dispute

s.m. A cow's ear; — a species of deer, Antilope picta (syn. nīl-gāʼo); — a mule; — a kind of serpent; — a span (from the tip of the thumb to that of the ring-finger); — a place of pilgrimage on the Malabar coast (sacred to Śiva); Śiva (as there worshipped)
(for go-karṇī), s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides, and its flower
s.m. The plant Asteracantha longifolia, and Ruellia longifolia, and Tribulus languinosus
s.m. A herd of kine; a multitude of cattle; a cow-station, cow-house; — a village or tract of the Jamna (the country around Vr̤indāvan, the residence of Krishṇ during his youth)
s.m. A measure of distance (being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard)
s.f. The side of a river, or a tank, &c. at which cows drink
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), Noxious to kine; — he who or that which kills kine; one who has killed a cow
s.m. 'Cow-heaven'; the heaven of Vishṇu, or of Krishṇa

vulg. gomtī, s.f. Name of a river in Northern India (it falls into the Ganges between Banāras and Gāzīpūr); — name of a Vedic hymn (a prayer or formula to be repeated during expiation for killing a cow); — cow-dung
or gau-mukh, s.m. A kind of musical instrument (a sort of horn or trumpet); — a hole in a wall (of a peculiar shape), made by thieves, &c.; a breach (syn. seṅdh)

or gau-mukhī, s.f. The chasm in the Himālaya mountains through which the Ganges flows (erroneously conceived to be shaped like a cow's mouth); — a cloth bag containing a rosary (the beads of which are counted by the hand thrust inside)
or gau-mutr, s.m. Cow's urine (which has to be imbibed in certain kinds of penance)

or go-medak, s.m. A gem or precious stone brought from the Himālaya and the Indus (described as of four sorts, — white, pale yellow, red, and dark blue); a kind of yellowish or tawny gem, a topaz; — go-meda-sannibha, s.m. Chalcedony or opal
s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow
s.m. (lit. 'having a nose like that of an ox'), A large kind of serpent, a boa-constrictor

s.m. lit. 'Nourisher of kine'; name of a celebrated hill in Vrindāvan, about fifteen miles to the west of Mathurā (it is the Parnassus of the Hindūs; and is said to have been lifted up and supported by Krishṇa upon one finger for seven days, to shelter the cowherds from a storm of rain sent by Indra to test Krishṇa's divinity; — hence Krishṇ is called Govardhan-dhar, and Giri-dhar, 'the hill-supporter')
s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

def: s. f. A vessel for holding milk, &c., a milkpail.
n. m. Sandy soil.
alternative form of گورْداسْپُور (gordāspūr, “Gurdaspur”)
Gurdaspur (a city and administrative headquarters of Gurdaspur District in the Indian state of Punjab); Gurdaspur District (a district of Punjab, India)
ex: لیکن پنجابی کا جو لہجہ سب کے لیے معیار قرار پایا، جس کی گرامر و اصول سب کے لئے قابل قبول ہوئے وہ لاہور، امرتسر، گورداسپور، سیالکوٹ اور ان کے نواحی علاقوں کا ہے۔
lekin panjābī kā jo lahja sab ke liye miʻyar qarār pāya, jis kī grāmar o aṣūl sab ke liy'e qābl-e-qabūl hū'e vo lāhaur, amritsar, gordāspūr, siyalkoṭ aur un ke nuvāḥī ʻilāqoṉ kā hai.
However, the dialect of Punjabi which was set as the standard for all, and whose grammar and principles became acceptable to all, belongs to Lahore, Amritsar, Gurdaspur, Sialkot and their suburbs.
wety: From گورداس (gordās) + ـپُور (-pūr, “town; city; village”). Named after Mahant Guriya das ji.

def: s.m. Fields near a village; homestead.
n. m. The homestead; fields near the village.
s.f. = goṛhā.

ety: goṛh˚ = goṛ˚ = koṛ = Prk. कोडे(इ) or कोड(इ) = S. कोटय(ते), rt. कुट्
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch; to dig, to excavate; to hollow out, scoop out.
v. n. S. गुहा a pit. To excavate; hollow; scoop out.
s.f. A kind of porcelain (which breaks the moment poison is put into it); — a plate.
more: s. f. A kind of porcelain, which breaks the moment poison is put into it.

def: s.f. (see gorā), A fair-complexioned woman; — a beauty, belle; a mistress.
more: n. f. S. ग्रुह take. Profit; gain. goṛī k. , v. a. 1. To earn; gain; make. Āj kyā goṛī kar lāë? 2. To bag; pocket. goṛī jamānā , v. n. See ṭippas jamānā. goṛī hāth se jānā , v. n. To lose one's prey. Bhaī! kyā goṛī hāth se gaī hai!

def: s.f. Taking away, carrying off; stealing; theft; — profit, gain, earnings; — plunder, booty, prey
more: s. f. Taking away. گوڙي کرنا goṛī karnā, To take away, to steal, to walk off with.
subs: v.t. To take away, carry off; to earn, make, gain; to walk off with, to steal
To lose (one's) plunder, or booty, or prey.

def: s.m. = P گورخر gor-ḵẖar, s.m. See s.v. gor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گورِسْتَان (gōristān). By surface analysis, گور (gor) + ـسْتَان (-stān).

def: badger; gravedigger
s.m. lit. 'Cow-keeper'; a Gorkhā, or inhabitant of Nepāl; (fig.) a short man (the Gorkhās being short).
more: n. m. 1. A Gorkhā . 2. A short man.
wety: Borrowed from Nepali गोर्खा (gorkhā, “(inhabitant of) Gorkha District”) named for गोरख (gorakh, “Gorakhnath”), from Sanskrit गोरक्ष (gorakṣa, “cowherd”).

def: s.m. = S گورستان gor-istān, s.m. See s.v. gor.
more: s. m. A wild ass, onager.

def: government

def: s.m. The ear (syn. kān)

s. m. The ear. گوشبند gosh-band, A bandage over the ears. گوش پيچ gosh-pech, An ornament worn in the turband. گوش دار gosh-dār, adj. Taking care, keeper, protector. گوش داري gosh-dār-ī, s. f. subs: s.m. A bandage over the ears

s. m. 1. A corner, an angle, a closet, cell, retirement, privacy. 2. Side. 3. Horn (of a bow). گوشي دار goshe-dār, adj. Angular. گوشه گزين gosha-guzīn, Fond of retirement. گوشه گير gosha-gīr, 1. ety: prob. for A. g̠aut̤, inf. n. of غوط 'to enter, to sink' (into
def: s.m. Sinking (into), diving; a plunge (into water), a dive; — consideration, deep meditation; — a swoon, fainting-fit
more: n. m. 1. Deep meditation ( gaur , 1). 2. A faint; swoon. Bachcha got meṅ paṛā hai. got mārnā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To dive; disappear. 2. To think deeply; go to the root of a matter. 3. To give oneself up to meditation. got meṅ ānā , v. n. To faint; swoon.
(from غوطه q. v.) s. m. Consideration. غوط مارنا g̠ẖot̤ mārnā, To disappear. غوط مين انا يا جا نا g̠ẖot̤ meṅ ānā or jānā, To faint.
subs: or g̠ot̤ mārnā (-meṅ), To plunge or dive (into); to disappear; to think deeply (on), be engaged in meditation (on), be absorbed (in)

ety: S. गो+त्रं, and त्रकं, rt. त्रै
def: s.m. Family, race, parentage, lineage, kith and kin, family stock; family name; a caste (collectively); subdivision of a tribe or caste; genus; class or species.
more: n. m.S. गौत्र 1. Lineage (اصل 5). 2. A family; a tribe. 1. Nāt kā, na got kā, bāṅṭā māṅge poth kā . Rus. Prov. Nor relative nor of the race, a share to ask he has the face. 2. Tamkhere sūṅ āp tamākhā seroṅ din din lāūṅgā, Gotī, mītbiṭhā-kar sagre hūkā dinon piyāūṅgā ! Rus. A seer of tobacco I'll fetch from the shop every day, With my brethren and friends gathered round me we'll all smoke away. 3. A sub-division of any common tribe or caste. Sāṛhe sattarah got ki lāj! Bards. The honor of seventeen and a half races rests with you! gotī, gotī-bhāī , n. m. One of the same family or stock; a kinsman. Jo moë dego chunnī bhussī, vāke rahūṅgo apnī khussī, Jo moë dego karrā jotā, vāko maryo kunbā gotā! Rus. The bullock's appeal. Who feeds me well with him I will remain, Who works me hard may all his kith be slain!
n. f. S. गुटिका 1. The border or edging of a garment. 2. A piece in chausar and pachchīsī , q. v. 3. A company. 4. ( Māṛ .) Feast; entertainment. 5. A village. goṭ bastī , n. f. A village site.
s. m. Parentage, lineage, pedigree, stock of a family.

def: s.m. Mustard-seed(?).

s.m. Edging; gold or silver lace (rather narrow); a narrow fillet of brocade; a preparation of coriander seeds, cocoa-nut, cardamoms, and coffee, &c. (in this sense perhaps from Prk. goṭṭha; S. goshṭha)
more: s. m. (gold or silver) Lace, or edging (rather narrow).
subs: s.f. Edging of gold or silver lace
s.m. A maker (or a seller) of edging, lace, braid, &c.

ety: prob. for A. غوطة g̠aut̤at, n. of un. fr. g̠aut̤; see g̠ot̤
def: s.m. Diving, plunging (into); dipping, immersion; a plunge (into water), a dive, a dip
more: n. m. A dip; dive; ducking. gotah denā , v. a. 1. To dip; plunge or duck under water. 2. To baptize. 3. To deceive ( dhokā d .). gotah khānā , v. n. 1. ( gota lagānā ) To be dipped; to dive. Chakrātī, taṛapti, phaṛphaṛātī, Gairat meṅ chalī woh gote khātī . The paper kite. Sohan Lāl. Circling, quivering, fluttering down Reeling with shame to earth anon. 2. To go astray; lose one's way. 3. To be tricked, cheated ( jul meṅ ānā ). Tū to chanchal, baṛī chatar hī, yāṅ kyoṅ gotā khāyā? Jāne nāhīṅ sāt hāth kī gharoṅ chhor-ke āyā! Jo tujh ko udmād lagā hā raṅḍvā koī na pāyā? Mere pīyā ke chhal meṅ ā gaī, yo haigā chhal- hāyā: Nigoḍī! nyānat-mārī? pīyā ne tohe bīsārī! Quarrel of co-wives. Play. So clever and so skittish! yet to be so grossly choused! What! knew you not a strapping wife was here already housed? When you became so very hot a widower could n't you get? Caught in my husband's shares! …

(from Pers. غوته) s. m. 1. Dipping, diving, immersion. 2. A dip, a dive, a plunge. غوطه باز g̠ẖot̤a-bāz, A diver. غوطه خور g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖor, A diver, any thing that dips, or is dipped. غوطه خورد g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖụrd, or غوطه خورده g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖụrda, Dipped, plunged. غوطه خوري g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. Dipping, being dipt. غوطه دينا g̠ẖot̤a-denā, To dip, to plunge under water. غوطه کهـانا g̠ẖot̤a-ḵẖānā, 1. To be dipt. 2. To dive. 3. To be deceived. 4. To travel in a miry road. 5. To stray, to wander, to lose oneʼs way. غوطه مارنا g̠ẖot̤a mārnā, To plunge. dive, dip. غوطي مين جانا g̠ẖot̤e meṅ jānā, To faint.
subs: vulg. g̠ot̤e-ḵẖor, s.m. A diver; — anything that dips or is dipped; a sort of fire-work (so called because, being lighted, it is thrown into water, when it dives, and every now and then shows above water)
or g̠ot̤a-ḵẖẉurda, part. adj. Plunged (into), dipped, immersed
vulg. g̠ot̤e-ḵẖorī, Dipping, diving; being dipped
(-ko), To plunge (into water), to dip, to duck; to baptize; — to deceive
v.n. To be dipped, to suffer immersion; to dive; — to be deceived; to travel in a miry or treacherous road; — to stray, wander, lose (one's) way
or g̠ot̤a mārnā (-meṅ), To plunge into, to dive; — to think deeply (on), enter deeply (into), be absorbed (in)

def: s.m. corr. of غوطه g̠ota, q.v.
unconsciousness
s.m. Name of a celebrated Rishi, author of several hymns of the Vedas; — name of a sage, the founder of the Nyāya philosophy
more: n. prop. The name of a muni, or saint, famous in Hindū mythology. He was the author of the Nyāya, or logick, and his doctrines corresponded with those of Aristotle. Gautama and his wife Ahalyā lived in a retired forest, where Indra, happening to pass, was struck with the beauty of the latter; and, assuming the appearance of Gautama, deceived and slept with his wife: which being discovered, Gautama cursed them both. To Indra he said, may your testicles fall to the ground; which accordingly took place: but Brahmā advised him to restore them with those of a ram, cut out for that purpose; hence, Indra is called मेषांडकोष mesh̤ānḍakosh̤a (ram-testicled). He wished that Ahalyā might be turned into stone; in which state she remained till Rūmachandra (son of Dasaratha ) passing that way with his brother Lachh̤maṇ, relieved her and restored her to her original form: and, being thus purified by Rā…
subs: (˚ma+ar˚), s.m. The forest in Central India in which Gotam Rishi practised his austerities
s.f. An epithet of Ahalyā the wife of Gotam Rishi (she was turned into stone by her husband for her adultery with Indra).

ety: for gūh; S. गूथं, rt. गू
def: s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
more: Sant. ī : n. f. S. गूथ Excrement ( bhishṭā ). 1. Gū dar gū, murgī kā gū . Prov. 2. Gū kā ṭokrā sir par uṭhātā hūṅ . Prov. A basket of filth I bear on my head. (Said of mean work or disgrace). gū uchhālnā , v. n. To bring disgrace upon oneself. gū se ghināonā k .; Bhoj. gūho seṅ ghīnāvan kail , v. a. To render one more despicable than filth. gū kā kīṛā , n. 1. A maggot formed in human excrements. Gū kā kīṛā gū hī meṅ khush rahtā hai . Prov. The maggot born of filth filth enjoys. gū k. , v. a. To dirty; spoil. gū khānā , v. n. 1. To eat filth. 2. To do a shameful act. 3. To tell a lie. gū mūt k. , Wom. v. a. To be a nurse to a child. gū mūṅh meṅ denā , v. a. To shame; heap reproaches on. gū h. , v. n. To be dirtied, spolit. guhāṅj'nī , n. f. A sty ( anjanhārī , 1).
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.m. A ball; anything round.

ety: v.n. fr. غبر 'to pass, or pass away,' &c.
def: s.m. Dust; clouds of dust; a dust-storm; vapour, fog, mist, mistiness; impurity, foulness; (met.) vexation, soreness, ill-feeling, rancour, spite; affliction, grief; perplexity; — the smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting ( = ḵẖat̤-ě-g̠ubār)
more: n. m. غبر was dust colored. 1. Clouds of dust; a dust storm. 2. Fog; mist. 3. Foulness; impurity. 4. Hatred ( bair , 1). 5. Soreness; bad blood ( chashmak ). 6. Affliction; grief. 7. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting. gubār-ālūdah . Covered with dust; dusty; begrimed. gubār nikālnā , v. a. 1. To give vent to passion. 2. To take revenge ( bair nikālnā ).

s. m. 1. Dust. 2. Vapour. 3. Clouds of dust, impurity, foulness. 4. ( met. ) Vexation, affliction, perplexity. 5. The smallest Arabic or Persian handwriting. غبار آلود g̠ẖubār-ālūd or غبارآلوده g̠ẖubār-ālūda, Covered or polluted with dust, dusty. غبار خاطر g̠ẖubār-i-ḵẖāt̤ir, Affliction, trouble of mind.
subs: (apnā), To give vent to (one's) ill-feeling, &c.; to take revenge.

def: s.m. A bomb; a mortar (for throwing shells); — a fire-balloon, a balloon.
more: n. m. A balloon, esp. a fire-balloon.
s. m. A bomb, a shell or mortar for throwing shells.

s.m. = گڙ guṛ, q.v. adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

s.m. (dialec.) = gūdā, q.v. s.m. The anus

s. m. Brain, marrow, kernel, pith, crumb.

s. (dakh.) See گڙاکو guṛ-ākū, and Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. lxxv.
s.m. = godām, q.v. s.m. A godown, warehouse; a store, heap; the stern of a boat (because stores, &c. are kept there).
v.t. To tattoo.
adj. Fat, plump; soft.
more: n. m. 1. Old cotton, esp. what is taken out of an old quilt. 2. ( gūdaṛ gādaṛ ) Old tattered clothes, or rags. gūdaṛ gāṅṭhnā , v. a. To stitch together tattered clothes. gūdaṛ meṅ giṅdauṛā, gūdaṛ kā lāl . A jewel in rags. Garīb ho gaë to kyā, ab bhī gūdaṛ ke lāl haiṅ .

def: s.m. A quilt; — old cotton (esp. what is taken out of an old quilt); a bundle of old clothes, or of rags and tatters; any old tattered clothes; — a kind of very soft silk stuff
more: s. m. 1. A quilt. 2. A bundle of old clothes. 3. Any old tattered clothes. 4. A kind of silk stuff. گودڙ خيل gūdaṛ-ḵẖail, adj. (applied only to females) Foolish, unmannerly, rude. گودڙ سينا gūdaṛ sīnā, To stitch together. گودڙ کالال gūdaṛ kā lāl or گودڙ مين گندوڙا نکلنا gūdaṛ meṅ gandauṛā nikalnā, Good proceeding from evil.
subs: adj. f. Foolish, silly; unmannerly, rude
or gūdaṛ-meṅ giṅdaurā, s.m. A jewel in rags; — good out of evil
s.m. Old tattered clothes; rags and tatters
or gūdaṛ sīnā, To stitch tatters together.

ety: i.q. gudṛī, by substitution of S. इकः for इका
def: adj. & s.m. Clothed in patched garments or in rags; — one so clothed, a tatterdemalion; a slovenly or shabby fellow; — one who sells or hires out quilts, tents, &c.
more: 1. adj. Clothed in patched garments or in rags; a tatterdemalion. 2. One who lets quilts for hire. گدڙياپير gudaṛiyā-pīr, A tree near a town or village on which people tie up rags in the manner of votive tablets, which they believe to be efficacious for the accomplishment of their wishes.
subs: s.m. A tree near a town or village (generally one which is supposed to be haunted) on which people tie up rags in the manner of votive tablets, which they believe to be efficacious for the accomplishment of their wishes.

def: n. 1. One clothed in patched garments or in rags; a tatterdemalion. 2. One who sells or hires out quilts, tents, etc. 3. A slovenly or shabby fellow.
more: n. m. S. गडुरिया. H. gādaṛ a sheep. A shepherd by caste or occupation. Ahīr dekhe gaḍaryā mastānā . Prov. The shepherd got drunk seeing the cowkeeper drunk. (The ahīr's is a higher caste). gaḍaryā-purān , n. m. A shepherd's tale. 2. Rustic lore. gaḍaryoṅ kā gīt , n. m. 1. A shepherd's song. 2. A pastoral ( ālā ).

def: v.n. corr. of guẕarnā, q.v. v.t. To cause to pass; to bring to pass; to pass (life, or time, &c.); to pass by; — to pass or lay before; to exhibit; to state.

part. adj. Melted, dissolved; — mild, gentle, affable; tender, soft; plump, stout; thick; — (in comp.) melting, dissolving; consuming; exterminating; — melter; refiner (e.g. dil-gudāz, 'heart-melting'; — z̤ulm-gudāz, 'exterminator of oppression'; — pl. gudāzān, 'melters; refiners'); — s.m. Melting; liquefaction; consumption; anguish.
more: adj. 1. Thick; plump; stout. 2. Melting; tender; soft. Dil gudāz hai .
(from گداختن) 1. adj. Melted, dissolved, mild, gentle, affable. 2. (in compos.) part. act. Melting, melter, dissolver. 3. sub. Liquefaction, melting, dissolution.

def: part. adj. Melting, dissolving; refining (gold).
more: part. act. (of گداختن) Melting, melter, dissolving.

subs: (-kā), To make an effigy (of); — to set up as an object of scorn.
ex:
Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
....گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمانی ہوگی شَگُن پِہ دیکھو شادمانی ہوگی ...
....guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmānī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

s. m. 1. A baby, doll. 2. A windgall. 3. Bough, branch.

To slap one on the nape of his neck. guddī se zabān nikālnā , v. a. To extract one's tongue. (A punishment in the East).

def: n. f. H. gūdaṛ . 1. A beggar's tattered garment or bedding; a ragged; quilt. 1. Pahnā libās khūb agar itr kā bharā, Yā chīthṛon kī gudṛī koī oṛh-kar marā . On death. Nazīr. Whether in robes that reek with perfume of the rose, Or in a ragged quilt the breath out of him goes? 2. Gar yār kī marzī huī sīr joṛ-ke baiṭhe, Ghar-bār chhuṛāyā to wahīṅ chhoṛ-ke baiṭhe, Moṛā jidhar us ne vahīṅ mūṅh moṛ-ke baiṭhe, Gudṛī jo silāī to use oṛh-ke baiṭhe, Aur shāl uṛhāyā to usī shāl meṅ khush haiṅ, Pūre haiṅ vohī mard jo har hāl meṅ khush haiṅ . Nazīr. If so my mistress willed I'd quiet sit, Or bade to leave my home by her I'd sit, Whichever way she turned me so I'd sit, Or made to sew a quilt, in it I'd sit, Or if wrapped in a shawl e'en so content, The perfect man is in each state content. 2. (Cor. of P. guzarī ) A daily market. 3. ( Slang ) Strength; power. Miyāṅ kī kyā gudṛī hai!
s.f. A daily market.
corr. of غدد g̠udad, or g̠udud, q.v.
s.m. A ball, &c. ( = gū or go, q.v.). s.f. Human excrement, ordure, dung, filth
subs: lit. 'To throw up dung'; to sully (one's own) good name, to bring disgrace upon oneself
(-ko), To make (one) more disgusting (or offensive) than dung, to render (one) more despicable than filth
To eat dung; — to do anything vile or shameful
(ke), 'To put dung on the face (of)'; to shame, to heap reproaches (or infamy) on one.

def: s.m. Forgiveness, pardon, remission of sins, absolution
more: (from غفر To cover, to forgive) s. m. Pardon, remission of sins. غفران پناه g̠ẖufrān-panāh, Asylum of pardon.

ety: for guptan; rt. old P. gub; see guft

inf. or verb. noun, To say, saying.

ety: guft, q.v.+tār = Zend tar; S. तार्
def: s.f. Speaking, saying, telling; speech, discourse, conversation; a saying; — a chapter.
more: n. f. گفتن to speak. Speech ( bāt , 3). Dastār aur guftār apnī hī kām ātī hai . Prov. Your turban your own hands must fold, your words must be your own.
(from گفتن) s. f. Speech, discourse, speaking, saying, telling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُفْتَار (guftār), from گفتن (“to say, tell”). By surface analysis, گُفْت (guft) + َار (ār).
